title: Chapter 5: Trial of Strength
paragraphs: The acid storm howled all night, but inside the protective barrier of the Observatory, a strange, fragile silence reigned. The five strangers didn't close their eyes. They sat in a tight circle, shoulder to shoulder, sharing the last of their warmth. Avotu watched them from his dark corner. He was struck by this connection. In a dying world where every survivor was ready to slit a brother's throat for a mouthful of clean water, these strangers from Yekaterinburg held onto each other with striking, almost holy devotion.
As soon as the first pale gray rays of the sick sun touched the ruins, the elf extinguished the barrier. Outside, a pungent fog wafted, hiding the mutilated earth.
"We're going with you," Alexander said firmly, rising to his shaky feet. "At home, we were just people with ordinary professions. Но здесь мы... in the same boat. We won't be a burden to you."
Avotu slowly nodded, saying nothing. The black flame on his head only swayed slightly.

They left the safety of the ruins and went deep into the ashy fog. They hadn't gone more than a couple of hundred steps when the ground beneath their feet suddenly rippled like water from a thrown stone. The air burst with a deafening crack, and a Sand Stalker—a massive, radiation-distorted centipede the size of a minibus, resembling a cross between a giant scorpion and a blind rotting wolf—erupted from under the ground. Slime dripped from its mouth, hissing corrosively on the ash.

Avotu reflexively reached for the Nature's Blade, but his brain suddenly failed. The trigger worked flawlessly. The smell of the monster's viscous slime was exactly the same as on the day Biotrix was torn apart. Time for the elf stopped. The air turned into thick jelly. His fingers, capable of bending steel, glided helplessly from the sword hilt. PTSD crashed down on him with the grace of a falling concrete slab. He froze in place, staring with wide eyes not at the monster, but into the void of his own cursed past.
The creature rushed at the paralyzed hero with a screech.
"Back!" Alexander rushed forward, raising the crossbow, but the bolt jammed tight.
Yuri stumbled and fell. The creature turned its ugly face to the nearest victim.

Ravan roared like a wounded bear. All the suppressed longing for his family, all his father's protective instinct turned into pure, blind rage. The former dentist, used to dominating physically, rushed to the side. He put all one hundred and twenty kilograms of muscle mass into one perfect blow, crashing into the creature's chitinous side. A vile, wet crunch echoed. Ravan flew back five meters with a desperate cry of pain. The bones of his right hand turned into crumbs. The animal power of human muscles meant nothing against this world.
"Ravan!!!" Evgeniy screamed at the top of his voice, rushing to his friend.
The scolopendra, irritated by the blow, loomed over the helplessly lying giant, its jaws open, ready to bite him in half.

Anar, standing behind, stopped breathing. He was always too lazy to argue, too lazy to run, too lazy even to think about the bad. But when the monstrous jaws hovered over his best friend, something inside the guy cracked irreparably. He didn't know spells. He didn't pray to the gods of this world. He simply categorically, with every pulse of his existence, refused to let Ravan die.

The air around Anar's outstretched arms exploded. It smelled of ozone so sharply that it stung the lungs. A blinding blue lightning bolt, thick as a ship's rope, tore from his fingers with a deafening roar that rent the heavens. A discharge of millions of volts struck the monster right in its gaping mouth. The creature screeched with an unearthly, grating voice, was lifted into the air and thrown back a dozen meters. The scolopendra's flesh smoked and blackened.

The crack of the discharge and the smell of roasted ozone tore Avotu out of his catatonia. The illusion of Biotrix vanished. The black flame on his head flared up maliciously, vengefully to the heavens. The elf took one elusive, blurred step forward. One dry, geometrically perfect stroke of the black blade—and the creature's severed head fell heavily into the ash.

Avotu lowered his sword. He inhaled heavily, wheezily, trying to calm the treacherous tremor in his palms. His bottomless eyes slowly shifted their gaze to the strangers. Yura and Zhenya were already kneeling around the pale, moaning Ravan, desperately trying to stop the blood. Alexander gripped Anar's shoulder, with chaotic, sparkling blue arcs of magic still running through his fingers.
None of the earthly people thought of fleeing. At that moment, Avotu realized a crushing thing: these people, even being absolutely defenseless, were stronger than him. They were alive.
